plan.title: Always include upgrade guidance section at the top of directive release notes

narratives.Description: Always include upgrade guidance section at the top of directive release notes
narratives.Overview:
# Always include upgrade guidance section at the top of directive release notes

## Problem

Users on older/stale directive installations frequently struggle to discover the correct upgrade path.

See the pinned issue: #1411 "Stuck on an old Deft version?"

Currently, when we cut a new `v0.x.y` release of the framework:
- The GitHub release notes are populated primarily from the promoted `CHANGELOG.md` section (via `scripts/release.py::_section_for_version` and the `gh release create` step in the release pipeline).
- There is no guaranteed, consistent "Upgrading from an older version" section at the top of the release notes.
- As a result, people who only look at the release they just got (or are considering adopting) often miss the canonical `deft-install --yes --upgrade --repo-root .` command and the associated guidance.

This makes the upgrade experience worse than it needs to be for the exact audience that needs help the most.

## Goal

For every official directive framework release (`deftai/directive`), the GitHub release notes should **always** start with a clear, consistent, and actionable "Upgrading from an older/stale version" section.

This section should be:
- At the very top of the release body (before the CHANGELOG content).
- Short and scannable.
- Link to the canonical guidance (#1411 and/or UPGRADING.md).
- Use the exact recommended command: `deft-install --yes --upgrade --repo-root . --json`

## Proposed Solution

Modify the release process (most likely in `scripts/release.py`, around the `create_github_release` function or the notes assembly logic in the pipeline) so that when releasing the directive framework itself, we prepend a standard banner block to the notes passed to `gh release create`.

Options to consider:
- Hard-code the banner for maintainer-mode releases of `deftai/directive`.
- Make the banner content live in a small template file (e.g. `.github/release-notes/upgrade-banner.md`) so it can be easily updated.
- Only apply this for directive framework releases (not when the release skill is used in consumer mode — see #1317).

We should also decide whether this banner should be injected into the promoted CHANGELOG section itself, or only into the GitHub release body (the latter is probably cleaner).

## Proposed Banner (starting point)

```markdown
## Upgrading from an older version?

If you are on an existing Deft installation and seeing warnings from `task doctor` about skill-path stubs, outdated surfaces, or payload staleness, run the canonical upgrade command:

```bash
deft-install --yes --upgrade --repo-root . --json
```

(Drop `--json` for human-readable output. Download the latest installer from the [latest release](https://github.com/deftai/directive/releases/latest) if needed.)

After upgrading, start a completely new agent session.

Full guidance: #1411
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every new directive framework release on GitHub has the upgrade section as the first content in the release notes.
- The section is easy to maintain / update over time.
- Does not affect consumer-mode usage of the release skill (see #1317).
- The change is documented in the release process / `deft-directive-release` skill.

## Related

- #1411 (pinned "Stuck on an old Deft version?" issue)
- #1317 (expanding release skill for consumer mode)
- Recent discussion in #1317 about changelog/release notes handling

---

**Status**: Proposal open for feedback before turning into implementation work.
narratives.Problem: GitHub release notes for deftai/directive are populated only from the promoted CHANGELOG section (scripts/release.py::_section_for_version, passed to create_github_release at the notes assembly point). There is no guaranteed 'Upgrading from an older version' section, so stale-install users miss the canonical deft-install --yes --upgrade --repo-root . --json command and the #1411 guidance.
narratives.Action: In scripts/release.py, prepend a standard upgrade-guidance banner to the GitHub release body (the `notes` value passed to create_github_release) for maintainer-mode releases of the directive framework only (repo == deftai/directive); do NOT inject it into CHANGELOG.md and do NOT apply it in consumer mode (#1317). Source the banner from a small editable template at .github/release-notes/upgrade-banner.md and fall back gracefully (skip banner, never fail the release) when the template is absent.
narratives.Test: tests/cli/test_release.py: assert the banner is prepended for a maintainer-mode repo, NOT prepended for a consumer-mode repo, and that a missing template degrades gracefully. Dogfood: v0.39.3 is the first release to carry the banner.

items.title: Every new directive framework release on GitHub has the upgrade section as the first content in the release notes.
The section is easy to maintain / update over time.
Does not affect consumer-mode usage of the release skill (see #1317).
The change is documented in the release process /  skill.
